description: Priority 1 - Move product catalog filtering/search/pagination to the server. Convert getProducts to accept filter params, create an API route for paginated fetches, and update ProductGrid to use React Query for infinite scroll instead of client-side filtering.
steps: 1. In app/products/page.tsx, modify getProducts to accept { category, q, sort, cursor, limit } parameters and apply where/orderBy/take/skip in the Prisma query. Keep unstable_cache with parameterized cache keys. The first page (24 items) should still be fetched server-side for SSR.

2. Create app/api/products/route.ts as a GET API route that accepts query params (category, q, sort, cursor, limit) and calls the same parameterized Prisma query. Return JSON with { items, nextCursor, total }.

3. Rewrite components/product-grid.tsx to: (a) Accept initial SSR items as props, (b) Use @tanstack/react-query useInfiniteQuery to fetch subsequent pages from /api/products as user scrolls or changes filters, (c) Remove the useMemo-based client-side filtering entirely, (d) Remove the setTimeout artificial delay - loading state should reflect real fetch, (e) Debounce the search input by 300ms before triggering a new query.
4. Wrap the app with a React Query provider if not already done (check if components/providers.tsx or app/layout.tsx already has one; if not, create one).
5. Update app/products/page.tsx to pass searchParams (category, q) to getProducts for the initial SSR fetch so the first page is already filtered.
6. Also return total count from the API so the UI can show 'X items found' accurately based on server data.
acceptance_criteria: Opening /products fetches only 24 products initially (not the full catalog)
Scrolling triggers API calls to /api/products for subsequent pages
Search input is debounced (300ms) before hitting the server
No artificial setTimeout delays remain in ProductGrid
Category/sort/price filter changes trigger new server queries
findings: React Query provider was already set up in components/providers.tsx wrapping the app. No additional provider setup needed. The build passes with prisma generate (no DATABASE_URL needed for type generation). Fallback to static products array works when DB is unavailable. Used cursor-based pagination with Prisma skip/cursor pattern. Variable naming conflict between Promise.all destructuring and fallback code was resolved by using dbTotal/fallbackTotal.
